# Robust Python (Patrick Viafore)

Before writing, modifying, or planning any code, you MUST use your `read_file` tool to load the relevant guidelines from `.roo/vendor/`. You may read multiple files if the task crosses domains.

## Table of Contents:
    - [Introduction to Robust Python](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/01-introduction-to-robust-python.md): This rule file is triggered whenever the AI is developing, refactoring, reviewing, or analyzing Python code. It specifically applies to tasks requiring data structure selection, iteration design, API signature creation, and general code maintainability improvements.
    - [Annotating Your Code with Types](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/02-annotating-your-code-with-types.md): Writing, modifying, refactoring, or reviewing Python code, specifically involving functions, classes, variables, public APIs, cross-module interfaces, and the setup of static analysis tooling and typecheckers.
    - [Introduction to Python Types](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/03-introduction-to-python-types.md): Trigger these rules when writing Python code, defining function signatures, declaring variables, designing domain models, or selecting data types to represent specific behaviors and constraints.
    - [Type Annotations](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/04-type-annotations.md): Python code development, specifically focusing on defining function signatures, applying type hinting, refactoring legacy code for static analysis, and ensuring typechecker (e.g., mypy) compliance to maintain clean and robust codebases.
    - [Constraining Types](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/05-constraining-types.md): Python codebases, specifically during tasks involving type hinting, data modeling, API design, refactoring legacy code, implementing error handling, defining state/configuration objects, or resolving typechecker (e.g., `mypy`) violations.
    - [Collection Types](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/06-collection-types.md): Python codebase modifications involving data collections, parsing heterogeneous data structures (like JSON, YAML, or CSVs), defining type annotations for lists/dicts/tuples/sets, extending or modifying collection behaviors, and designing generic data structures.
    - [Customizing Your Typechecker](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/07-customizing-your-typechecker.md): Python development tasks involving type hinting, static analysis configuration, typechecker optimization, security flaw detection (taint analysis), and codebase diagnostic setups using tools like mypy, Pyre, and Pyright.
    - [Adopting Typechecking Practically](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/08-adopting-typechecking-practically.md): Trigger these rules when adopting, retrofitting, or managing Python typechecking in an existing codebase, specifically when strategizing which files to annotate, managing legacy code (brown-field projects), or utilizing automated type-generation tools like MonkeyType, Pytype, and mypy.
    - [Defining Your Own Types](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/09-defining-your-own-types.md): Defining data structures, modeling domain concepts, creating classes, structuring configuration data, refactoring primitive collections (like dictionaries or tuples) into robust types, or establishing data validation boundaries in Python.
    - [User-Defined Types: Enums](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/10-user-defined-types-enums.md): The domain encompasses data modeling, refactoring variables that accept a constrained set of values, defining state machines, handling configuration choices, replacing magic strings or numbers with named constants, and implementing bitwise flags/multi-select options in Python.
    - [User-Defined Types: Data Classes](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/11-user-defined-types-data-classes.md): The modeling and implementation of Python data structures, domain entities, and object-oriented data containers for heterogeneous data groupings and business logic, specifically in cases without complex interdependent invariants.
    - [User-Defined Types: Classes](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/12-user-defined-types-classes.md): Creating, modifying, or refactoring Python data structures, user-defined types, object-oriented designs, or classes; specifically deciding how to model data and behaviors, enforcing state constraints, defining class APIs, or organizing methods versus module-level functions.
    - [Defining Your Interfaces](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/13-defining-your-interfaces.md): Designing, refactoring, or extending Python interfaces, APIs, public-facing classes, modules, or any boundaries where different parts of a codebase (or different developers) interact.
    - [Subtyping](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/14-subtyping.md): Python object-oriented design, focusing on class hierarchies, inheritance, mixins, interface implementation, composition, and duck typing, specifically regarding the definition of relationships between types and the enforcement of the Liskov Substitution Principle (LSP).
    - [Protocols](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/15-protocols.md): Python type hinting, interface design, resolving typechecker errors related to duck typing or dynamic behavior, refactoring inheritance hierarchies, and decoupling dependencies between producers and consumers using structural subtyping.
    - [Runtime Checking With pydantic](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/16-runtime-checking-with-pydantic.md): Parsing, validating, and modeling dynamic or external data (e.g., from configuration files, network requests, databases, JSON, YAML) in Python codebases where static typechecking is insufficient to guarantee runtime data integrity.
    - [Extensible Python](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/17-extensible-python.md): The domain encompasses tasks related to designing system architecture, adding new features to existing codebases, refactoring highly coupled or "spaghetti" code, implementing notification/event systems, designing plug-in frameworks, and resolving complex dependency chains or repeated code modifications across multiple files (shotgun surgery).
    - [Extensibility](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/18-extensibility.md): This rule file is triggered whenever the user requests the AI to add new features to an existing system, refactor code for future growth, modify core business logic to support new workflows, resolve tight coupling/spaghetti code, or explicitly asks for implementation of the Open-Closed Principle (OCP), extensibility, or SOLID principles.
    - [Dependencies](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/19-dependencies.md): Architectural design, refactoring, dependency management, package resolution, module import structuring, system design, or writing code that interacts with internal or external libraries in a Python codebase.
    - [Composability](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/20-composability.md): Writing, refactoring, or reviewing Python code, specifically when tasked with designing system architecture, creating reusable libraries, abstracting logic, or when requested to make code 'composable', 'extensible', or 'reusable'.
    - [Event-Driven Architecture](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/21-event-driven-architecture.md): Architecting or implementing event-driven systems in Python, decoupling components into producers and consumers, working with publish/subscribe (pub/sub) message brokers, implementing the Observer pattern, or building reactive data pipelines/streams.
    - [Pluggable Python](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/22-pluggable-python.md): Creating extensible Python architectures, refactoring tightly coupled systems, designing algorithms with customizable steps, injecting entire algorithms dynamically, or building/managing plug-in architectures (specifically using the `stevedore` library or `setuptools` entry points).
    - [Building a Safety Net](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/23-building-a-safety-net.md): Creating, modifying, evaluating, or refactoring Python tests, CI/CD pipelines, static analysis configurations, quality assurance workflows, or test automation scripts.
    - [Static Analysis](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/24-static-analysis.md): Python codebase maintenance, static analysis configuration, continuous integration (CI/CD) setup, code complexity reduction, security scanning, and the development of custom abstract syntax tree (AST) linters.
    - [Testing Strategy](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/25-testing-strategy.md): Creating, modifying, planning, or refactoring Python tests, specifically utilizing the `pytest` framework, or defining an overall testing strategy for a Python codebase.
    - [Acceptance Testing](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/26-acceptance-testing.md): Gathering product requirements, designing test strategies, configuring continuous integration test reports, generating behavioral specifications, writing and maintaining acceptance tests, and interacting with Behavior-Driven Development (BDD) paradigms, the Gherkin specification language, and the Python behave testing framework.
    - [Property-Based Testing](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/27-property-based-testing.md): Property-Based Testing (Generative Testing) in Python using the Hypothesis library, specifically applied when traditional parameterized tests are brittle, when testing complex algorithms, when evaluating stateful objects, or when manual boundary value analysis is insufficient.
    - [Mutation Testing](.roo/vendor/python/robust-python-patrick-viafore/28-mutation-testing.md): Python codebases possessing existing test suites where test effectiveness, robustness, and the quality of the testing safety net need to be evaluated, validated, or improved.
